Classify the request before drafting so the page structure fits the change.

Choose feature when:
- The main value is new behavior, a new workflow, or a meaningful enhancement.
- Readers need to understand capabilities, usage, and dependencies.

Choose bugfix when:
- The main value is correcting incorrect behavior, eliminating a regression, or reducing a failure mode.
- Readers need the problem, root cause, and validation path.

Choose general change when:
- The request spans feature work, refactoring, operational setup, rollout, or project planning.
- No single feature or bugfix outline covers the material cleanly.

If the request mixes types:
- Pick the dominant change driver.
- Borrow one or two sections from another outline instead of merging every section.
- Prefer a shorter accurate page over a bloated comprehensive one.
